entries: rusted iron lantern half-buried in roots
thick smoke clings lower oak boughs
cool damp air under largest oak boughs
threads: why smoke thickens near black oak
find dripping sound source in woods

places.0,0: The room features a rough-hewn wooden door scarred by weather, dry brittle leaves scattered on stone floor, and dense shadowed path leading left with dripping water source.
places.1,0: A narrow ravine between dry shale and a thicket of stunted black oaks, with stone steps leading upward. Thin smoke drifts over the ridge to the west.
